components.name: MOPS (3-(N-morpholino)propanesulfonic acid)
components.evidence: MOPS minimal medium (Wanner, 1994) was used for most of the experiments... [Neidhardt MOPS 10x mixture: MOPS adjusted to pH 7.4 with KOH, final 40 mM]

components.name: Tricine (N-[tris(hydroxymethyl)methyl]glycine)
components.evidence: Tricine adjusted to pH 7.4 with KOH [component of the Neidhardt MOPS 10x buffer mixture, final 4 mM]

components.name: NH4Cl (ammonium chloride)
components.evidence: 50 mL of 1.90 M NH4Cl [per 10x MOPS mixture; final 1x concentration 0.0095 M]

components.name: K2HPO4 (dipotassium phosphate)
components.evidence: K2HPO4 stock 0.132 M ... Final 1X 0.00132 M [added separately to the MOPS base; sole phosphate source]

components.name: K2SO4 (potassium sulfate)
components.evidence: 10 mL of 0.276 M K2SO4 [per 10x mixture; final 1x 0.276 mM] — sole sulfate source

components.name: CaCl2 (calcium chloride)
components.evidence: 10 mL of 5.0 x 10^-4 M CaCl2 [per 10x mixture; final 1x 5 x 10^-4 mM]

components.name: NaCl (sodium chloride)
components.evidence: 100 mL of 5.0 M NaCl [per 10x mixture; final 1x 0.05 M]

components.name: FeSO4.7H2O (ferrous sulfate)
components.evidence: 10 mL of .01 M FeSO4 [per 10x mixture; final 1x 1 x 10^-5 M = 0.01 mM]

components.name: CoCl2 (cobalt chloride)
components.evidence: Micronutrients ... CoCl2 [Neidhardt final 3 x 10^-8 M = 30 nM]

components.name: MnCl2 (manganese chloride)
components.evidence: Micronutrients ... MnCl2 [Neidhardt final 8 x 10^-8 M = 80 nM]

components.name: D-Xylose
components.evidence: cells were grown in 50 ml of MOPS medium supplemented with 0.2% xylose ... the doubling time of the purified talAB-knockout strain on minimal medium containing xylose as the sole carbon source was confirmed to be 110 min

components.name: O2 (oxygen)
components.evidence: growth of a wild-type strain on non-fermentative carbon sources, succinate and acetate ... indicated that the oxygen supply was sufficient to support aerobic growth; MFA cultures grown in 500 ml Erlenmeyer flasks (shaken)

media.base_medium: Neidhardt MOPS minimal medium (Neidhardt, Bloch & Smith 1974), used as in Wanner 1994

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as 'MOPS minimal medium (Wanner, 1994)' = Neidhardt et al. 1974 J Bacteriol 119:736-747
sources_chased.how: WebSearch + WebFetch genome.wisc.edu/resources/protocols/mopsminimal.htm and ASM journals.asm.org reproduction of Neidhardt 1974 Table
added: NH4Cl 9.5 mM (nitrogen source, was mislabeled as molybdate on EX_nh4_e)
6 micronutrients: molybdate 3 nM, H3BO3 400 nM, CoCl2 30 nM, CuSO4 10 nM, MnCl2 80 nM, ZnSO4 10 nM

missing_before: NH4Cl (ammonium chloride) — the actual nitrogen source; the EX_nh4_e exchange in current_components was mislabeled as '(NH4)6(Mo7)24 (molybdate)'
explicit compound identities/concentrations for K2HPO4 phosphate, K2SO4, MgCl2, CaCl2, FeSO4
media.notes: Standard defined Neidhardt/Wanner MOPS minimal medium with D-xylose (0.2%) as the sole carbon source; grown aerobically at 37C. Concentrations are the canonical Neidhardt 1974 final 1x values; the six micronutrients already present as ion exchanges are confirmed, and the key correction is that ammonium chloride (nitrogen) had been mislabeled as ammonium molybdate. No casamino acids in this sole-carbon medium (those were used only in the 96-well plate screen); no anaerobic reductant (aerobic culture).
